mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence increases.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong consumer spending supports economic growth
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat (mean return < 0.01%) over any 2-year rolling window, despite positive growth in RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which can lead to increased wage pressures and higher operating costs for corporations, eventually eroding profit margins and negatively impacting equity valuations over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Tight labor markets can increase wage inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of very low federal funds rates and subdued inflation signals an accommodative monetary policy environment. This reduces the cost of capital for businesses and boosts investor risk appetite, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and mining activity, signaling strong underlying economic momentum. This positive economic signal typically boosts short-term market sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where industrial production shows accelerating growth during an expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts reflects growing consumer and business confidence, indicating a healthy and expanding economy. This fundamental strength supports higher equity valuations over a longer investment horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently for 6 consecutive months, but SPX 60-day forward returns are negative during that period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, leading to increased consumer spending and corporate profits. This positive economic backdrop supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are consistently negative despite strong PAYEMS growth exceeding 150,000 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly low or declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) can signal weakening aggregate demand or disinflationary pressures, which may lead to lower future corporate pricing power and reduced earnings expectations, resulting in negative short-term market reactions.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL shows significant disinflationary pressure.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth combined with strong industrial production indicates a healthy and expanding economy with both strong demand and supply-side activity. This provides a strong fundamental basis for positive equity returns over a longer period.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth > 2.5% AND INDPRO annual growth > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDP and industrial production are key economic health indicators
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are consistently flat or negative when both GDP and industrial production show strong growth for two consecutive quarters.
